message:
test(fcp-conformance/streaming-health): pin StreamHealthTracker state-machine causality (br-841ir)

StreamHealthTracker drives connector health classification across all
streaming connectors with a documented state machine:

  Connected -(heartbeat overdue, after heartbeat_timeout)-> Degraded
  Degraded  -(zombie, after zombie_timeout)-> Unhealthy
  Degraded  -(heartbeat received)-> Connected (recovery)
  Connected -(record_disconnect)-> Reconnecting
  Reconnecting -(at max attempts)-> Unhealthy
  Reconnecting | Unhealthy -> no automatic transitions; needs reset()

Inline tests in fcp-streaming exercise these transitions but no
cross-crate conformance pinned them. A regression that fired
Connected -> Degraded AT (instead of strictly after) the timeout, or
that let evaluate() walk OUT of Unhealthy automatically, would
silently corrupt every connector's health telemetry.

Adds 11 tests against the public fcp_streaming::StreamHealthTracker
API (no fake clock — uses 30/90 ms timeouts so the suite still runs
in 0.16s):

- fresh_tracker_starts_in_connected_state
- record_heartbeat_keeps_state_connected
- missed_heartbeat_after_timeout_transitions_connected_to_degraded
- heartbeat_after_degraded_recovers_to_connected (recovery causality)
- zombie_timeout_transitions_degraded_to_unhealthy
- evaluate_does_not_automatically_recover_from_unhealthy (critical
  invariant — record_heartbeat alone does NOT walk Unhealthy back)
- record_disconnect_transitions_to_reconnecting_under_max_attempts
- record_disconnect_at_max_attempts_transitions_to_unhealthy
- record_reconnected_returns_to_connected_and_clears_attempts (the
  reconnect_count reset, otherwise sticky)
- reset_returns_to_connected_from_any_state (operator escape hatch)
- snapshot_reports_state_consistent_with_state_method

Also adds fcp-streaming as a fcp-conformance dev-dep.

All 11 pass locally in 0.16s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
streaming_health_state_machine_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
